items.title: Home prices hit a new all-time high this summer
items.description: Home prices continue to climb even as buyers retreat from the market, according to fresh data from the National Association of Realtors.

items.title: ‘I’ve no idea how they got her number’: My student-loan servicer called a friend after I missed a payment. Is that legal?
items.description: “She said it’s the second message they have left.”

items.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
items.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

items.title: ‘I get $1,460 in Social Security’: My millionaire ex-husband, 74, refuses to pay alimony. What can I do?
items.description: “I have very little money, while my ex’s financial statements show assets in the millions.”

items.title: Schwab strategists warn of a major market shift: The era of easy index gains is officially over
items.description: More geopolitical instability, more frequent supply shocks and macro volatility are facing investors in an era that’s just starting, says Charles Schwab.

items.title: The U.S. and China are today’s leaders in AI. These countries could be the next-stage champions.
items.description: Economists at Bank of America spotlight South Korea and the United Arab Emirates as likely to gain from both the build-out and adoption of artificial intelligence.

items.title: Salesforce’s stock may look like a bargain — but that’s just a mirage, analyst says
items.description: KeyBanc downgraded Salesforce shares, citing doubts about the company’s AI narrative.

items.title: Major drugmaker loses $27 billion in market value after failed trial
items.description: Two companies announced on Thursday that the heart-disease drug they had been developing failed its Phase 3 trial.

items.title: Gen Z-ers share how they’re achieving financial freedom — and the old ideas they’re leaving behind
items.description: A stable job and a nice home have become less accessible to young Americans. Many are taking new paths to pursue their dreams.

items.title: PepsiCo cuts prices on snacks. It’s not enough to drive growth in North America.
items.description: PepsiCo’s stock was set to fall as the company’s North America business lagged, but strength in international business led to an earnings beat.
